page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The system is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, though cached small-block read bandwidth and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Database operations and static web serving throughput also register in the lower 25% tier, while Redis SET operations achieve an average of 5.76 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high-performance dedicated CPU profile relative to its resource footprint, making it a viable option for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory allocations. However, the lack of local storage and lower memory-to-CPU ratio represent clear tradeoffs. This server is best suited for single-threaded compute tasks, lightweight web serving, batch processing, and moderate Redis caching workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz on x86_64 architecture. Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, this KVM-based instance does not include local storage or a GPU, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 10% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. Conversely, database operations and static web serving throughput are weaker, landing in the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the instance highly suitable for compute-heavy, single-threaded workloads, batch processing, and moderate caching applications where high memory density is not required.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance delivering top-tier single-core performance for processing-heavy workloads without local storage requirements.

8.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for single-threaded compute, batch processing, and caching
